title: Revisionist History
author: Pushkin Industries
why: Pushkin's flagship launch title and one of the most consistently top-charting podcasts since 2016; helped establish Pushkin as a network in 2018.
description: Malcolm Gladwell revisits overlooked, misunderstood, or forgotten events and people from the past, reargued each episode with contrarian analysis and archival reporting.

title: Cautionary Tales
author: Tim Ebl & Ryan Hanson
why: Long-running Pushkin flagship with dozens of internationally chart-topping episodes; frequent Apple/Spotify top-10 placement.
description: Economist and journalist Tim Harford narrates dramatized true stories of disasters, scams, and errors in judgment, drawing lessons from history's biggest mistakes.

title: The Staten Island Problem
author: Pushkin Industries
why: Spun out as a standalone feed from Revisionist History due to strong listener response.
description: A five-part limited series reconstructing Staten Island's early-1990s secession movement against New York City, woven through the rise of Giuliani, the peak of the city's homicide rate, and a young Donald Trump.

title: Against the Rules
author: Pushkin Industries
why: One of Pushkin's chart-topping flagship titles since the network's founding.
description: Narrative-journalism series from author Michael Lewis examining fairness, expertise, and institutional trust through deeply reported seasons on referees, coaches, and the live-event ticketing industry.

title: Broken Record
author: KMIH 889 The Bridge
why: Long-running flagship blending narrative music journalism with A-list guest access.
description: Music documentary and in-depth artist storytelling from Rick Rubin, Malcolm Gladwell, Bruce Headlam, and Justin Richmond, with narrative deep-dive episodes on artists, songs, and musical eras alongside interviews.

title: Deep Cover
author: Pushkin Industries
why: Praised anthology format with celebrity narration; consistently ranked among Pushkin's top true-crime titles.
description: Reported true-crime anthology from Pulitzer Prize-winning journalist Jake Halpern going inside undercover investigations, con games, and disappearances.

title: Fiasco
author: Pushkin Industries
why: Named one of TIME's 100 Best Podcasts of All Time.
description: Leon Neyfakh's narrative history series reconstructs American political scandals and crises episode by episode using deep archival tape and reporting, showing how chaos and cover-ups unfolded in real time.

title: The Last Archive
author: Pushkin Industries
why: Acclaimed for archival sound design and Lepore's historian-led narrative approach.
description: Harvard historian and New Yorker writer Jill Lepore hosts a narrative history show investigating how Americans know what they know — tracing the decline of shared truth through cases spanning espionage, science, and media.

title: Lost Hills
author: Western Sound and Pushkin Industries
why: One of Pushkin's earliest forays into true crime; well-reviewed reported journalism format.
description: New Yorker writer Dana Goodyear investigates dark true-crime cases hidden beneath Malibu's wealthy, celebrity-driven surface.

title: Deep Cover: Never Seen Again
author: Pushkin Industries
why: Standalone-charting season within the Deep Cover anthology.
description: Season 3 of Deep Cover follows two women on opposite coasts who went missing in the summer of 1999, unraveling the parallel mysteries of their disappearances.

title: The Chinatown Sting
author: Pushkin Industries
why: Part of Pushkin's fall 2025 slate, produced with the network's renewed iHeartMedia partnership.
description: Investigative docuseries hosted by Lidia Jean Kott reconstructing a notorious undercover drug bust in Manhattan's Chinatown in the late 1980s and its long fallout.

title: McCartney: A Life in Lyrics
author: iHeartPodcasts and Pushkin Industries
why: High-profile Pushkin/iHeart co-production tied to McCartney's published lyrics anthology.
description: Paul McCartney and poet Paul Muldoon go song-by-song through McCartney's catalog, blending memoir, narrative history, and songwriting masterclass to reconstruct pivotal moments in Beatles and solo history.

title: Empire City: The Untold Origin Story of the NYPD
author: Audible | Crooked Media
network: Wondery (Malcolm Gladwell's Pushkin-affiliated Ivron/Third Ear, distributed via Wondery)
why: Gladwell-affiliated narrative history project examining policing's evolution; distributed via Wondery, so early episodes/ad-free access sit behind Wondery+.
description: A narrative history of the New York Police Department from its founding through the present, tracing how policing, corruption, and reform have shaped the city.

title: Legal Docket
author: WORLD Radio
why: Narrative legal-history entry in Pushkin's growing documentary slate.
description: Narrative retellings of pivotal U.S. Supreme Court cases, dramatizing the human stories and legal arguments behind landmark rulings.

title: Blindspot: The Road to 9/11
author: The HISTORY® Channel and WNYC Studios
network: History Channel / WNYC Studios (Pushkin-adjacent, Jim O'Grady)
why: Acclaimed narrative history series; a WNYC/History Channel co-production (not a confirmed Pushkin title), free on all major apps.
description: Narrative investigative history tracing the decades of missed warnings, intelligence failures, and geopolitical missteps that preceded the September 11 attacks.

title: Dr. Death
author: Audible
why: One of Wondery's flagship true-crime hits; adapted into a Peacock scripted series starring Joshua Jackson.
description: Narrative investigation into surgeon Christopher Duntsch, whose botched operations maimed and killed patients while a hospital system failed to stop him; later seasons cover other medical-fraud cases.

title: Dirty John
author: LA Times Studios
why: Massive breakout hit that helped launch Wondery; adapted into a Bravo/Netflix scripted anthology series.
description: Adapted from Christopher Goffard's LA Times investigation, the flagship season follows con man John Meehan as he infiltrates and nearly destroys a Southern California family; the anthology continued with other real cons and cases.

title: The Shrink Next Door
author: Audible | Bloomberg
why: Adapted into an Apple TV+ limited series starring Will Ferrell and Paul Rudd.
description: Journalist Joe Nocera investigates his own former neighbor's psychiatrist, Dr. Isaac Herschkopf, who allegedly manipulated a patient for decades, taking over his business, home, and social circle.

title: Business Wars
author: Audible
why: One of Wondery's longest-running franchises, credited with popularizing the corporate-rivalry narrative format.
description: Narrated by David Brown, each season dramatizes a corporate rivalry — Nike vs. Adidas, Netflix vs. Blockbuster, Coke vs. Pepsi — tracing the strategy, egos, and stakes behind the fight for market dominance.

title: WeCrashed
author: Audible
why: Adapted into an Apple TV+ series starring Jared Leto and Anne Hathaway.
description: Chronicles the meteoric rise and collapse of WeWork under founder Adam Neumann, examining the cult of personality and financial recklessness behind a $47 billion valuation that evaporated.

title: Over My Dead Body
author: Audible
why: Season 1 was optioned for TV development; 'Joe vs. Carole' rode the Tiger King wave.
description: An anthology of true stories about ordinary relationships that spiral into murder, from a bitter custody battle to the Joe Exotic/Carole Baskin feud.

title: The Apology Line
author: Audible
description: The story of Allan Bridge's anonymous NYC 'Apology Line' answering machine, which became an obsession that consumed his life — told partly by his widow, Marissa Bridge.

title: Even the Rich
author: Audible
why: Long-running Wondery culture franchise with millions of downloads per season.
description: Narrative deep dives into wealthy families and celebrity empires — Kardashians, Murdochs, Vanderbilts — examining how fortunes were built and fractured.

title: American Scandal
author: Audible
why: One of Wondery's flagship narrative-history franchises.
description: Lindsay Graham narrates deep dives into pivotal American scandals — Enron, Iran-Contra, Theranos, Watergate — exploring what drives people to break the rules and what happens when they're caught.

title: American History Tellers
author: Audible
why: Flagship Wondery history franchise with dozens of multi-part series.
description: Narrative history covering pivotal American eras and events — the Cold War, Prohibition, the Space Race, the Civil Rights Movement — tracing how they shaped the present.

title: British Scandal
author: Audible
description: Hosts Alice Levine and Matt Forde narrate infamous British scandals — political, royal, and celebrity — tracing the lie at the center of each and its fallout.

title: Scamanda
author: Lionsgate Sound
why: Became one of Wondery's top-charting shows of 2023.
description: Investigates Amanda Riley, a Christian blogger who faked a years-long battle with cancer to defraud her church community and online followers of hundreds of thousands of dollars.

title: Do You Know Mordechai?
author: USG Audio
description: Journalist Kathleen Goldhar investigates Mordechai Horowitz, a charming figure who defrauded friends and family within Toronto's Orthodox Jewish community.

title: Ghost Story
author: Audible | Pineapple Street Studios
why: Won Best Documentary Podcast at the 2024 Ambies; nominated for Best True Crime at the 2024 British Podcast Awards.
description: Host Tristan Redman investigates ghost sightings in his childhood bedroom, which lead him to uncover his own family's connection to an unsolved decades-old murder next door.

title: Kill List
author: Audible | Novel
description: Tech journalist Carl Miller discovers a murder-for-hire 'kill list' hidden on the dark web and races against time to warn the hundreds of people targeted before police act.

title: Suspect
author: Audible | Campside
why: One of Campside's founding hits; praised for its deep dive into touch-DNA forensics and prosecutorial overreach.
description: Journalists Matthew Shaer and Eric Benson unravel how a homeless San Jose man, Lukis Anderson, was nearly convicted of a murder he didn't commit after his DNA was innocently transferred to the crime scene by paramedics.

title: RedHanded
author: RedHanded
why: Consistently ranks among the top true-crime podcasts globally.
description: Hosts Hannah Maguire and Suruthi Bala research and narrate true-crime cases ranging from notorious serial killers to obscure disturbing mysteries and cults.

title: Against the Odds
author: Audible
description: Dramatized true survival stories — a Thai cave rescue, a Somali pirate hostage crisis, plane crash survivors — narrated as immersive first-person accounts.

title: This Is Actually Happening
author: Audible
description: First-person narrated true stories of extraordinary, life-altering experiences — near-death events, cult escapes, disasters — told directly by the people who lived them.

title: The Vanished
author: Narrative.fm
description: Host Marissa Jones investigates real missing-persons cases through interviews with family, investigators, and witnesses, tracing the circumstances of each disappearance.

title: Killer Psyche
author: Audible | Treefort Media
description: Retired FBI profiler Candice DeLong narrates and analyzes notorious criminal cases through the lens of criminal psychology, exploring what drove the killers to act.

title: Sports Explains the World
author: Audible | Meadowlark
description: Narrative essays connecting famous moments in sports history to broader political and cultural turning points, tracing how sport and history intertwine.

title: Serial
author: Serial Productions & The New York Times
network: Serial Productions (This American Life / WBEZ, later The New York Times)
why: First podcast ever to win a Peabody Award; hundreds of millions of downloads; widely credited with launching the modern narrative-podcast boom and helping spur Adnan Syed's case back into the courts.
description: Long-form investigative narrative journalism that returns each season to a real case, opening with Sarah Koenig's re-investigation of the 1999 murder of Hae Min Lee and the conviction of Adnan Syed.

title: S-Town
author: Serial Productions
why: Peabody Award winner; over 40 million downloads in its first week, frequently cited as one of the best-reviewed podcasts ever produced.
description: What starts as a request to investigate a murder in rural Alabama unspools into a sprawling character study of John B. McLemore, a brilliant, tormented clock restorer, and the town he both loved and despised.

title: This American Life
author: This American Life
why: Nine-plus Peabody Awards and the first Pulitzer Prize ever given to a radio/audio program; the direct progenitor of Serial and Serial Productions.
description: Weekly themed anthology combining reported narrative journalism, first-person testimony, and audio verité; the show whose reporting DNA and staff seeded Serial and Serial Productions.

title: Nice White Parents
author: Serial Productions & The New York Times
network: Serial Productions / The New York Times
why: Widely praised for reframing the failure of school integration as a story about power and whiteness rather than policy alone; major critical acclaim on release.
description: Chana Joffe-Walt traces the history of one Brooklyn public school across generations to show how the desires and demands of white parents have repeatedly shaped, and undermined, efforts at school integration.

title: The Trojan Horse Affair
author: Serial Productions & The New York Times
network: Serial Productions / The New York Times
why: Major awards nominee; notable for the unusually candid on-air friction between its two reporters over the story's framing. (Corrected from subscription-only: released free on all major podcast apps in 2022, prior to NYT Audio's subscription-only model.)
description: Hosts Brian Reed and Hamza Syed re-investigate an anonymous letter that alleged an Islamist plot to take over Birmingham, UK schools, unraveling the moral panic and ruined careers it triggered.

title: The Coldest Case in Laramie
author: Serial Productions & The New York Times
network: Serial Productions / The New York Times
why: Praised for its rigorous, self-interrogating approach to cold-case journalism, including scrutiny of the reporter's own hometown biases. NYT Audio subscription required.
description: Pulitzer-winning investigative reporter Kim Barker returns to her Wyoming hometown to re-examine a 1985 unsolved murder and the shaky 2016 arrest of a suspect decades later.

title: The Good Whale
author: Serial Productions and the New York Times
network: Serial Productions / The New York Times
why: Widely covered as a surprising, emotionally rich animal-welfare narrative from Serial's team, expanding the studio's subject range beyond crime. NYT Audio subscription required.
description: Daniel Alarcón traces the real-life story behind Keiko, the orca who played the star of Free Willy, and the costly, well-intentioned campaign to release him back into the wild.

title: The Retrievals
author: Serial Productions / The New York Times
network: Serial Productions / The New York Times
why: Peabody Award winner; frequently cited on best-podcasts-of-2023 lists for its restrained, devastating storytelling. NYT Audio subscription required.
description: Investigation into a fertility clinic where patients underwent painful egg-retrieval procedures without adequate anesthesia after a nurse was found diverting fentanyl, examining medical negligence and dismissed patient pain.

title: Animal
author: National Park Service
why: NYT Audio's lyrical, essayistic entry into nature writing as narrative audio, distinct from the studio's crime/institutional-investigation output. NYT Audio subscription required.
description: Writer Sam Anderson travels the world meeting individual animals — an octopus, an owl, a pigeon — to explore what looking another creature in the eye reveals about consciousness and connection.

title: Caliphate
author: The New York Times
why: Won the first Peabody Award given to a Times-produced podcast, but became a landmark cautionary case in narrative audio after the central source's claims collapsed, prompting an extensive editors'-note retraction and return of a Peabody nomination.
description: Rukmini Callimachi reports on ISIS's rise and recruitment, centered on a Canadian man's claims of having joined and killed for the group in Syria.

author: The New York Times
why: Audio companion to the Pulitzer Prize-winning 1619 Project; widely taught, debated, and cited in national conversations about how slavery is remembered.
description: Nikole Hannah-Jones and Times journalists trace how the legacy of slavery, beginning with the 1619 arrival of enslaved Africans in Virginia, shaped American democracy, capitalism, music, land ownership, and healthcare.

title: Rabbit Hole
author: The New York Times
why: Early, influential narrative examination of algorithmic radicalization that helped shape mainstream coverage of platform-driven extremism.
description: Tech columnist Kevin Roose follows individuals whose lives and beliefs were reshaped by YouTube's recommendation algorithm to examine how online radicalization actually happens.

title: Day X
author: The New York Times
why: A rare NYT narrative series reported largely from Germany, tracing far-right infiltration of state security institutions.
description: Investigates a cache of stolen ammunition and a covert network inside Germany's police and military allegedly preparing for "Day X" — the collapse of German democracy — while also revisiting a decade of unsolved neo-Nazi murders.

title: The Last 12 Weeks
author: Serial Productions / The New York Times / The Marshall Project
network: Serial Productions / The New York Times / The Marshall Project
why: A 2025 collaboration between Serial Productions and The Marshall Project, extending the studio's death-penalty and wrongful-conviction reporting into real-time, high-stakes narrative. Feed access is genuinely unclear: recent Serial Productions/NYT releases have increasingly shifted toward gating within the NYT Audio app rather than a fully open RSS feed.
description: Death-penalty reporter Maurice Chammah embeds with the defense team racing against the clock to stop the execution of David Wood, convicted in 1992 of murdering women and girls near El Paso, Texas.

title: 99% Invisible
author: Roman Mars
why: Founding flagship show of Radiotopia; multiple Webby and Edward R. Murrow Awards; one of the most influential and longest-running narrative podcasts in public radio.
description: Roman Mars narrates deeply researched stories about the unnoticed architecture, design, and infrastructure that shapes everyday life, from urban planning failures to the hidden history of everyday objects.

title: Radio Diaries
author: Radiotopia / PRX
why: Multiple Peabody Awards; pioneered the first-person audio diary format later adopted across public radio.
description: Peabody Award-winning producer Joe Richman hands tape recorders to ordinary people and lets them document their own lives, plus produces first-person 'hidden histories' recovered from archival tape.

title: Ear Hustle
author: Radiotopia / PRX / San Quentin
why: First podcast created inside a prison; two-time Peabody Award nominee and Pulitzer Prize finalist for Audio Reporting.
description: Co-hosted by Nigel Poor and formerly incarcerated Earlonne Woods (later Rahsaan 'New York' Thomas), the show tells first-person stories of daily life inside and after prison, produced in part inside San Quentin State Prison.

title: The Loop
author: Ear Hustle & Radiotopia
why: New Fall 2025 Radiotopia series extending Ear Hustle's Peabody-nominated reporting tradition.
description: A spinoff from the Ear Hustle team continuing narrative reporting on incarceration, reentry, and the systems that keep people cycling through prison.

title: The Kitchen Sisters Present
author: Radiotopia / PRX
why: Multiple Peabody Awards and a MacArthur-adjacent reputation for pioneering feature-documentary sound art on public radio over four decades.
description: Davia Nelson and Nikki Silva produce lush nonfiction sound-portraits from the margins of history — lost recordings, hidden subcultures, secret kitchens, and people possessed by an obsession or mission.

title: Love + Radio
author: Marquze Killion
why: Repeatedly named one of the best podcasts ever made by Vulture and The Atlantic for its radical, unmediated interview-collage style.
description: Nick van der Kolk builds intimate, formally experimental character studies from raw interview tape — con artists, extremists, outsiders, and people living at the edge of ordinary morality — without narration to soften the story.

title: Articles of Interest
author: Avery Trufelman
why: Spun off from 99% Invisible into its own acclaimed Radiotopia show; Season 2 became a bestselling book.
description: Avery Trufelman reports narrative-history deep dives into clothing and fashion — school uniforms, Hawaiian shirts, the fabric of American identity — tracing how what we wear encodes power and culture.

title: Criminal
author: Vox Media Podcast Network
why: One of the original and most acclaimed narrative crime podcasts; multiple Webby Awards and a devoted critical following since 2014.
description: Long-running narrative anthology exploring people who have done wrong, been wronged, or been caught somewhere in the middle, ranging far beyond typical true-crime tropes into legal and cultural oddities.

title: This Is Love
author: Vox Media Podcast Network
why: Sister show to Criminal from the same acclaimed production team.
description: From the makers of Criminal, narrative-documentary stories of sacrifice, devotion, and obsession — investigating love in its many, sometimes unsettling, forms.

title: Mumbai Crime
author: Goldhawk
why: Part of Radiotopia Presents, the network's home for premiering artist-owned limited series.
description: John Scott Dryden and Ayeesha Menon report immersive true-crime narrative journalism recorded on the streets of Mumbai, following real investigations and the detectives who worked them.

title: Proxy
author: Marita Ellingsen
why: New Radiotopia series from a former Invisibilia co-host, welcomed to the network in 2025.
description: Yowei Shaw (formerly of Invisibilia) pairs people facing a personal dilemma with a stranger who's lived through the closest possible version of it, following the resulting conversation as narrative documentary — described as 'emotional investigative journalism.'

title: 70 Million
author: LWC Studios
why: Acclaimed criminal-justice-reform narrative journalism distributed through PRX.
description: Mitzi Miller hosts narrative journalism examining the U.S. money bail system and pretrial detention through the stories of people caught inside it.

title: Uncuffed
author: KALW
why: Peabody Award-winning; one of the few narrative shows fully reported and produced from within the prison system.
description: Narrative documentary journalism produced by incarcerated and formerly incarcerated reporters inside California state prisons, telling first-person stories of life on the inside.

title: Song Exploder
author: Hrishikesh Hirway
why: Adapted into a Netflix documentary series; a Webby and Podcast Award winner and one of Radiotopia's most popular shows.
description: Musicians deconstruct one of their own songs track by track, narrating the creative and technical choices behind it in their own unmediated voice — presented as pure narrative with no host questions audible.

title: The Shadows
author: CBC
why: From the creator of The Heart, praised for its formally daring first-person documentary style.
description: Kaitlin Prest (The Heart) narrates an intensely personal documentary series tracing patterns of intimacy, heartbreak, and family inheritance across her own relationships.

title: Strangers
author: Ibn Bashiir & Slaveofmostwise
why: One of the network's original founding shows at Radiotopia's 2014 launch alongside 99% Invisible and Love + Radio.
description: Lea Thau narrated intimate true stories of chance encounters, connection, and estrangement between people — one of Radiotopia's seven founding shows.

title: Snap Judgment
author: Snap Judgment and PRX
why: Long-time PRX-distributed narrative storytelling institution; multiple Peabody Award nominations.
description: Glynn Washington hosts a narrative storytelling anthology of first-person true stories — mystery, romance, adventure, and tragedy — told with a distinctive music-and-beat-driven sound design.

title: The Polybius Conspiracy
author: Jon Frechette, Todd Luoto
why: Distinctive Radiotopia Presents limited series blending internet-folklore mystery with reported journalism.
description: A limited-run narrative-mystery investigation into the urban legend of 'Polybius,' a supposedly mind-control-linked 1980s arcade game, following the reporter as she tracks down the truth behind an internet myth.

title: Embedded
author: NPR
why: Named one of Time's 100 Best Podcasts; consistently ranked among top documentary journalism podcasts.
description: NPR's flagship documentary strand — each season a producer goes deep on one story, from gun violence aftermath to abortion access to political extremism, told scene-by-scene with tape from the ground.

title: Invisibilia
author: NPR
why: Long-running NPR flagship narrative show; ended in 2023 budget cuts after nearly a decade.
description: Narrative science-and-storytelling show fusing personal stories with research on the unseen forces (belief, categorization, emotion) that shape human behavior.

title: Rough Translation
author: NPR
why: Peabody-nominated international narrative journalism; canceled in NPR's 2023 layoffs.
description: Followed familiar American conversations and debates into unfamiliar territory abroad, reported internationally with a narrative, story-first approach.

title: Believed
author: NPR
why: Peabody Award winner; widely credited with shaping national reporting on the Nassar case.
description: Investigative series on the Larry Nassar sexual abuse scandal within USA Gymnastics, following the survivors who exposed him and the institutional failures that enabled decades of abuse.

title: White Lies
author: NPR
why: Peabody Award winner; major critical acclaim for cold-case civil-rights reporting.
description: Two Alabama journalists reinvestigate the unsolved 1965 murder of civil-rights activist Rev. James Reeb, uncovering decades of buried evidence and lies.

title: Buried Truths
author: WABE
why: Hosted by a Pulitzer Prize–winning historian; praised for meticulous historical reconstruction.
description: Pulitzer-winning journalist Hank Klibanoff digs into cold cases of racial violence and injustice in the Jim Crow-era American South.

title: Louder Than a Riot
author: NPR
why: NPR Music's first narrative podcast; widely praised, ended in 2023 layoffs.
description: Traces the intertwined rise of hip-hop and mass incarceration in America, following artists whose careers collided with the criminal justice system.

title: Radio Ambulante
author: My Cultura, Radio Ambulante and iHeartPodcasts
why: First Spanish-language show to win a Peabody Award (2019); longest-running Spanish narrative journalism podcast.
description: Longform narrative journalism in Spanish telling character-driven stories from across Latin America and Latino communities, covering everything from crime to migration to everyday life.

title: Throughline
author: NPR
why: Multiple Webby and Signal Award wins for historical storytelling.
description: Weekly narrative-history journalism that traces the historical roots of current events, blending archival tape, expert interviews, and scripted narration.

title: The Kids of Rutherford County
author: Serial Productions & The New York Times
network: WPLN (Nashville Public Radio) / ProPublica / Serial Productions
why: Built on Peabody/Murrow-winning ProPublica investigative reporting; widely acclaimed 2023 release.
description: Investigates how a Tennessee juvenile court judge and officials illegally jailed hundreds of children, told through the families who lived it.

title: The Line
author: Apple TV / Jigsaw Productions
why: Acclaimed embedded war-reporting narrative on military justice
description: Investigates Navy SEAL platoon chief Eddie Gallagher's war crimes trial and the culture that enabled it, reported by a former SEAL-turned-journalist.

title: Motive
author: WBEZ Chicago
why: Peter Lisagor Award and RFK Journalism Award winner
description: Investigative anthology series that goes deep inside a single true story each season to answer 'how could this have happened,' spanning wrongful conviction, disappearance abroad, and community anti-violence work.

title: Trump, Inc.
author: WNYC Studios
why: Joint WNYC/ProPublica investigative project with ongoing reader/listener-sourced reporting
description: Open-investigation narrative podcast examining Donald Trump's business empire and conflicts of interest during his presidency.

title: The Gun Machine
author: 건박
why: Won in the Podcast category at the 2024 Edward R. Murrow Awards
description: Investigates the origins and rise of the AR-15 rifle in America and how it became central to mass shootings and gun culture.

title: Alabama Astronaut
author: Abe Partridge & Ferrill Gibbs
network: Independent / NPR member-station adjacent (Alabama Public Radio distribution)
why: Breakout indie-to-public-radio crossover praised for immersive field recording and storytelling
description: Musician Abe Partridge and a preacher travel Appalachia recording obscure snake-handling and holiness-church congregations, weaving music discovery with religious narrative.

title: Sold in America
author: Stitcher & E.W. Scripps Company, Noor Tagouri
description: Investigates the underground economy and legal gray zones of sex work and trafficking in the U.S., told through first-person narrative reporting.

title: Chameleon
author: Audiochuck | Campside Media
network: Campside Media / iHeartPodcasts (early seasons via Stitcher)
why: Widely acclaimed true-crime anthology; Hollywood Con Queen season was adapted for TV/film development and topped charts. Early ad-free episodes require an iHeartPodcasts+ (formerly Stitcher Premium) subscription.
description: An anthology of jaw-dropping true impersonation and deception stories, starting with the Hollywood Con Queen who spent years posing as powerful female executives to scam industry hopefuls.

title: Sympathy Pains
author: iHeartPodcasts
why: From the reporter behind Dr. Death; praised for its psychological depth on Munchausen-style deception.
description: Hosted by journalist Laura Beil (Dr. Death), this six-part series unravels the story of Sarah Delashmit, a woman who fabricated cancer, illness, and tragedy to manipulate the people around her.

title: Unfinished Justice
author: Yda
description: A ten-part series that goes inside the NYPD's Cold Case Squad, following detectives as they reopen and attempt to solve some of the department's most haunting unsolved cases.

title: Spectacle: True Crime
author: Sony Music Entertainment
why: Part of Neon Hum's Spectacle vertical, named to Best Of lists by The Atlantic, Time, and Esquire.
description: A twelve-part narrative series hosted by Mariah Smith that turns the lens on true crime itself, examining the genre's biggest cases alongside America's obsession with consuming them.

title: Dirt Cheap
author: Sony Music Entertainment
description: A serialized narrative noir production dramatizing the novel Murder in the Glass Room, following a misanthropic bookie-turned-detective through 1970s Los Angeles.

title: Fake Priest
author: Sony Music Entertainment
why: One of Neon Hum's founding narrative titles.
description: An early Neon Hum narrative investigation into a man who posed as a Catholic priest, exploring how he deceived congregations and institutions.

title: The Sellout
author: Cameron Bradford
description: A narrative investigation produced by Carla Green digging into a case of deception and betrayal, told in Neon Hum's signature documentary style.

title: Hell and Gone
author: iHeartPodcasts
why: School of Humans' original breakout hit and longest-running narrative title.
description: School of Humans' flagship true-crime series in which host Catherine Townsend digs into small-town unsolved murders and disappearances across the American South, revisiting the same case across multiple seasons.

title: Rumble: Ali/Foreman and The Soul of '74
author: iHeartPodcasts
why: Commemorated the fight's 50th anniversary with archival reporting and cultural history.
description: A narrative history series hosted by investigative journalist Zaron Burnett revisiting the 1974 Rumble in the Jungle between Muhammad Ali and George Foreman and the historic Zaire '74 music festival that surrounded it.

title: Queen Havoc and Her Murder Cult
author: iHeartPodcasts
description: A ten-part narrative series hosted by filmmaker Kurt Kubicek following Cecilia Steyn, dubbed the 'Charles Manson of South Africa,' and the murder cult she orchestrated.

title: Racket
author: School of Humans / iHeartPodcasts
why: Named a Vulture 'Best New True Crime Podcasts' pick.
description: A narrative investigative series unpacking a scheme of organized fraud or corruption, reported and scripted in School of Humans' documentary style.

title: Sanctum Unmasked
author: iHeartPodcasts
description: A narrative deep dive into Hollywood's infamous 'Sanctum' sex club, examining the power dynamics and secrecy surrounding it.

title: Bad Seeds
author: iHeartPodcasts
description: An Earth Month narrative series from School of Humans exploring an environmental mystery or scandal through reported, scripted storytelling.

title: Red Elvis
author: iHeartPodcasts
network: School of Humans / iHeartPodcasts / Curiosity Stream
description: A narrative history podcast telling the strange true story of Dean Reed, an American rock musician who became a communist-era celebrity in the Eastern Bloc.

title: Long Shot
author: School of Humans / iHeartPodcasts
description: A narrative documentary series from School of Humans reconstructing an underdog or against-the-odds true story through scripted reporting.

title: The Turning
author: iHeartPodcasts and Rococo Punch
why: Season 1 won the Livingston Award for International Reporting, the first podcast to do so.
description: An investigative narrative series hosted by Erika Lantz probing closed, high-control communities, beginning with women who left Mother Teresa's religious order and expanding to the insular world of ballet and a Minnesota cult.

title: Hollywoodland
author: Hollywoodland
network: Double Elvis / Exactly Right Media / iHeartPodcasts
why: Spin-off of Disgraceland; fully scripted, sound-designed single-narrator storytelling.
description: A scripted narrative anthology from Jake Brennan and Zeth Lundy chronicling Hollywood's darkest true stories: murder, kidnapping, organized crime, curses, and disappearances tied to the film industry.

title: Disgraceland
author: Double Elvis / Exactly Right Media / iHeartPodcasts
network: Double Elvis / Exactly Right Media / iHeartPodcasts
why: Won the iHeartRadio Music Award for Best Podcast (2020); 260+ episode archive.
description: A single-narrator scripted series hosted by Jake Brennan exploring musicians' true crimes and scandals, from Amy Winehouse to the Grateful Dead, blending music history with true-crime reporting.

title: Tales From Hollywoodland
author: Double Elvis / iHeartPodcasts
description: A companion narrative series extending Hollywoodland's scripted true-crime storytelling into further tales of Hollywood scandal and intrigue.

title: Monster: The Zodiac Killer
author: Tenderfoot TV / iHeartPodcasts
why: Follow-up season to the hit Atlanta Monster; part of the acclaimed Monster franchise.
description: A 15-episode narrative investigation into the unsolved Zodiac Killer case, hosted by Matt Frederick and Payne Lindsey, tracing the murders and communal terror across Northern California in 1968–69.

title: Behind the Bastards
author: Cool Zone Media and iHeartPodcasts
why: Multiple Webby Award nominations; one of Cool Zone Media's flagship titles, borderline narrative given occasional guest-banter format.
description: Host Robert Evans delivers deeply researched, single-narrator-led historical exposés on history's worst tyrants, fraudsters, and cult leaders, going well past the Wikipedia summary into original reporting.

title: Infamous
author: Campside Media / Sony Music Entertainment
why: Spawned the standalone spinoff Allison After NXIVM, widely covered by entertainment press in 2025. Ad-free early access sits behind a paid tier.
description: Journalists Vanessa Grigoriadis and Gabriel Sherman (and later Natalie Robehmed) go behind the scenes of the biggest tabloid-scale scandals of the last two decades, from sex cults to explosive divorces.

title: White Devil
author: Campside Media
why: Campside Media's first wholly self-owned original series; covered by Deadline as a flagship launch. Ad-free early access sits behind a paid tier.
description: Host Josh Dean investigates the shooting death of senior Belizean police officer Henry Jemmott by Canadian property developer Jasmine Hartin, unpacking a story of expat privilege, a hidden empire, and a death still shrouded in mystery.

title: Witnessed: Night Shift
author: Sony Music Entertainment / Campside Media
why: Investigates one of the largest suspected undetected serial-killer cases tied to a U.S. federal hospital.
description: Journalist Jake Adelstein (author of Tokyo Vice) returns to his Missouri hometown to investigate a 1992 spike in overnight patient deaths at a VA hospital, and whether a nurse present at nearly every code blue was a serial killer the government covered up.

title: Go-Boy!
author: iHeartPodcasts
why: Praised for blending true-crime storytelling with a literary redemption arc; distributed under Campside's Binge-style early-access model despite the iHeart partnership.
description: Hosted by Sam Mullins, the series traces the legendary criminal career of Roger 'Mad Dog' Caron — 78 bank robberies and 13 prison escapes across Canada's brutal mid-century penitentiaries — and his unlikely redemption as an award-winning author.

title: Big Time
author: Apple TV / Campside Media
network: Apple Original / Campside Media / Piece of Work Entertainment
why: Apple Original hosted by actor Steve Buscemi; anthology format drew attention for its starry host and wide-ranging capers. Feed note: Apple Podcasts exclusive, no open RSS on other apps.
description: Hosted by Steve Buscemi, an anthology series delivering a new true story each week about audacious cons, heists, and schemes that almost worked — from fake kidnappings to a stolen shark.

title: Shadow Kingdom: God's Banker
author: Crooked Media & Campside
why: Explores one of the most enduring unsolved Vatican-linked financial scandals of the 20th century.
description: Investigates the 1982 death of Roberto Calvi — 'God's Banker' — found hanging from a London bridge after threatening to expose the Vatican Bank's criminal ties, and the decades-long quest by lawyer Nicolo Majnoni to uncover the truth behind the ruled 'suicide.'

title: Reply All
author: Gimlet
why: One of the most acclaimed and influential narrative podcasts of the 2010s, multiple Webby Awards; the "Test Kitchen" investigation sparked an internal controversy that contributed to the show's 2022 end.
description: A show "about the internet" that mixed narrative reported stories — internet mysteries, scams, tech culture deep-dives — with the hosts' own investigations, including a story that turned into a reckoning with the show's own internal culture.

title: Crimetown
author: Gimlet
why: One of Gimlet's flagship true-crime hits; helped define the modern city-by-city organized crime documentary format.
description: Deep-dive narrative documentary series examining organized crime and civic corruption in a different American city each season, built from hundreds of interviews with criminals, cops, and politicians.

title: Heavyweight
author: Pushkin Industries
why: Beloved for its emotional craft and humor; consistently ranked among the best narrative podcasts of its era.
description: Jonathan Goldstein helps guests travel back to the pivotal moment when a relationship or decision in their life went wrong, and tries to help them resolve it.

title: StartUp
author: Y Combinator
why: The founding show of Gimlet Media; credited with popularizing the behind-the-curtain company-building documentary format.
description: The show that launched Gimlet Media, opening with a season narrating founder Alex Blumberg's own real-time struggle to build the company, then continuing with narrative profiles of other startups and small businesses.

title: Uncivil
author: Gimlet
why: Episode "The Raid" won a Peabody Award for its co-hosts.
description: Narrative history series digging into overlooked and mythologized stories from the American Civil War, reported from the vantage of marginalized voices.

title: The Habitat
author: Gimlet
why: Praised for turning a scientific psychology experiment into gripping serialized nonfiction drama.
description: A real-time documentary following six volunteers living inside a simulated Mars habitat on a Hawaiian volcano for a NASA-funded isolation study.

title: The Clearing
author: The Clearing Podcast
why: Reached #1 on Apple Podcasts.
description: Docuseries about April Balascio, who as an adult realized her own father, Edward Wayne Edwards, was a serial killer and turned him in.

title: Stolen
author: Spotify Studios
why: First podcast to win both a Pulitzer Prize (Audio Reporting) and a Peabody Award. Corrected access: despite Gimlet's Spotify ownership, the show is distributed free on all major podcast apps, not Spotify-exclusive.
description: Investigative journalist Connie Walker reports on missing and murdered Indigenous people and institutional abuse across North America, with each season a distinct investigation.

title: Missing Richard Simmons
author: Topic / Pineapple Street Media / Dan Taberski
why: One of Pineapple Street's first breakout hits; #1 on Apple Podcasts; sparked debate over consent and ethics in podcast journalism.
description: Personal investigative narrative into fitness icon Richard Simmons' abrupt disappearance from public life, exploring celebrity, isolation, and fandom.

title: Wind of Change
author: Pineapple Street Studios / Crooked Media / Spotify
network: Pineapple Street Studios / Crooked Media / Spotify
why: Widely acclaimed genre-bending investigative series; a Pineapple/Crooked/Spotify co-production nominated for major podcast awards. Corrected access: released simultaneously on Crooked Media's free feed and Spotify, not Spotify-exclusive.
description: Journalist Patrick Radden Keefe investigates a persistent Cold War-era rumor that the CIA secretly wrote the Scorpions power ballad "Wind of Change" as covert propaganda.

author: Pineapple Street Studios | Amazon Music | Wondery
why: Won three Ambie Awards including Podcast of the Year (2022).
description: Dan Taberski's series about ordinary Americans navigating the surreal, permanently altered country in the days and years after September 11, 2001.

title: Running from Cops
author: Topic / Pineapple Street Media / Dan Taberski
why: Peabody Award nominee (2020).
description: Dan Taberski's critical examination of the reality TV show Cops and its three-decade influence on American perceptions of policing.

title: Surviving Y2K
author: Topic / Pineapple Street Media / Dan Taberski
why: From the Missing Richard Simmons team; praised for blending comedy with reporting.
description: A look back at the Y2K panic, separating what really happened from the myth as the world braced for computers to fail at the millennium.

title: Project Unabom
author: Apple TV+ / Pineapple Street Studios
why: Detailed insider account of one of the longest manhunts in FBI history.
description: An in-depth look at the FBI's hunt for the Unabomber, told through interviews with the agents who caught him, Ted Kaczynski's brother, and Kaczynski's own writings.

title: My Fugitive
author: Pineapple Street Studios and Audacy
description: Documentary series about a woman's decades-long entanglement with a man wanted by the FBI.

title: Stay Away from Matthew MaGill
author: Pineapple Street Studios and Audacy
description: Documentary investigating a charismatic figure named Matthew MaGill and the trail of people whose lives he upended.

title: This Land
author: This Land That I Love
why: Award-winning reporting that broke ground ahead of the McGirt v. Oklahoma Supreme Court ruling.
description: Cherokee journalist Rebecca Nagle investigates a Supreme Court case over tribal sovereignty in Oklahoma, then reports on how the far right exploits Native child-welfare cases to advance a broader legal agenda.

title: Missing America
author: Missing America
description: Documentary series on how the United States abdicated its role as a global example for democracy and human rights during the Trump years.

title: Gaining Ground: The New Georgia
author: Gaining Ground: The New Georgia
description: Documentary on the organizers, strategists, and voters who made Georgia's historic 2020–2021 Senate flips possible.

title: Welcome to Your Fantasy
author: Pineapple Street Studios / Gimlet
network: Pineapple Street Studios / Gimlet Media / Crooked Media (Spotify)
why: Widely praised true-crime/pop-culture crossover; Spotify-exclusive three-network co-production.
description: Traces the rise and lurid fall of the Chippendales male-stripper empire and its founder Somen 'Steve' Banerjee, blending business history with culture and crime.

title: Firebug
author: truth.media
network: Truth Media (Marc Smerling) / Sony Music Entertainment
why: From a creator of The Jinx; praised for its unusual literary angle on a true-crime investigation.
description: Investigates a decade of unsolved arson attacks that killed four people in Southern California and the strange literary trail — a novel manuscript — that led investigators to the real culprit.

title: Radiolab
author: WNYC Studios
why: Multiple Peabody Awards; one of the most influential and widely imitated narrative-audio shows ever made.
description: Peabody Award-winning show that turns big science and philosophy questions into deeply reported, sound-designed narrative documentaries, often following a single investigative thread across an episode or season.

title: More Perfect
author: WNYC Studios
why: Peabody Award winner; widely cited as a model for narrative legal journalism.
description: Narrative documentary series unpacking landmark U.S. Supreme Court cases through the human stories, politics, and consequences behind them.

title: La Brega
author: Futuro Media
why: Ambie and Webby-recognized; praised for bilingual, bicultural storytelling.
description: Bilingual narrative documentary series exploring Puerto Rican identity, history, and crisis through seven defining stories and songs.

title: Blindspot
author: The HISTORY® Channel and WNYC Studios
why: Peabody-nominated; Tulsa Burning widely acclaimed for historical reconstruction of the Greenwood massacre.
description: Anthology of deeply reported narrative-history documentaries; seasons have reconstructed the road to 9/11, the Tulsa Race Massacre, and the opioid crisis inside Purdue Pharma and the Sackler family.

title: Caught
author: WNYC Studios
why: Praised for humanizing systemic failure in youth incarceration through first-person narrative.
description: Narrative documentary series investigating the American juvenile justice system through the lived experience of the kids caught inside it.

title: There Goes the Neighborhood
author: WNYC Studios and KCRW
network: WNYC Studios with KPCC/LAist, WAMU, WFAE, KALW
why: Multi-market public-radio collaboration; award-nominated for its ground-level approach to housing policy.
description: City-by-city narrative documentary series examining gentrification and displacement told through residents' own stories, with separate seasons set in Brooklyn, Los Angeles, the Bay Area, and Charlotte.

title: Dead End: A New Jersey Political Murder Mystery / Dead End: Crime and Politics
author: WNYC, Nancy Solomon
why: Hosted by veteran WNYC reporter Nancy Solomon; widely reviewed true-crime/political hybrid.
description: True-crime narrative investigation into the unsolved double murder of a politically connected New Jersey couple, with a second season tracing Sen. Bob Menendez's corruption and its espionage entanglements.

title: The Experiment
author: The Atlantic and WNYC Studios
why: Cross-publication collaboration between WNYC Studios and The Atlantic magazine.
description: Narrative documentary anthology examining the contradictions of the American experiment through intimate, deeply reported stories.

title: Spooked
author: KQED and Snap Studios
why: One of the most popular horror-adjacent narrative-nonfiction podcasts.
description: Spinoff anthology of true supernatural and paranormal personal-experience narratives, told in Snap Judgment's cinematic storytelling style.

title: Lost Notes
author: KCRW
why: Hosted across seasons by Jessica Hopper and Hanif Abdurraqib; widely praised narrative music journalism.
description: Anthology music-history documentary series unearthing overlooked or mythologized true stories from pop-music history, with each season built around a theme or era.

title: Bodies
author: KCRW, Allison Behringer
why: Praised for its intimate, journalistically rigorous approach to women's and reproductive health.
description: Documentary series following one woman's real, unresolved medical mysteries, using each case to interrogate blind spots in the healthcare system.

title: The Document
author: KCRW
why: KCRW's flagship documentary-format narrative show.
description: Documentary series that mashes up first-person tape and reported narrative to tell real, present-tense stories as they unfold.

title: Question Everything
author: Brian Reed
network: KCRW (hosted by Brian Reed, creator of S-Town)
why: From the creator of S-Town; applies novelistic narrative technique to media criticism.
description: Narrative documentary series examining journalists' fraught relationships with the stories they cover and the ethical costs of reporting the truth.

title: 16 Shots
author: WBEZ Chicago
why: Built on WBEZ's award-finalist investigative reporting; widely acclaimed accountability journalism.
description: Narrative documentary reconstructing the Chicago police killing of Laquan McDonald and the city hall cover-up of the dashcam video that followed.

title: Making Obama
author: WBEZ Chicago
why: Hosted by Jenn White; critically praised origin-story documentary.
description: Narrative history series tracing Barack Obama's rise from Chicago community organizer to U.S. senator, built from original interviews and archival tape.

title: Making Oprah
author: WBEZ Chicago
why: Hosted by Jenn White; one of WBEZ's most downloaded narrative series.
description: Narrative history series charting Oprah Winfrey's rise from local Chicago news anchor to global media mogul.

title: Beyond All Repair
author: WBUR
why: Produced with Zac Stuart-Pontier (Crimetown, The Jinx) as lead editor; widely reviewed 2024 breakout true-crime series.
description: Ten-episode cold-case documentary re-investigating a decades-old murder near Boston and the woman convicted of it, from a three-year reinvestigation led by WBUR's Amory Sivertson.

title: Endless Thread: Madness
author: WBUR
why: From WBUR's Endless Thread team; the reporting seed that grew into Beyond All Repair.
description: Five-part narrative documentary investigating alleged crimes at a prestigious New England institution with ties to the CIA's MKUltra program, told through the internet-mystery lens of Endless Thread.

title: Kind World
author: WBUR
why: Long-running WBUR narrative strand distributed nationally on public radio.
description: Short-form narrative documentary series telling true stories of unexpected kindness, generosity, and human connection.

title: Imperfect Paradise
author: LAist Studios
why: LAist Studios' flagship narrative series, hosted by Antonia Cereijido.
description: Weekly longform narrative journalism series digging into the biggest, most consequential Southern California stories, from housing fights to an activist's death to a sheriff's-department scandal.

title: Norco '80
author: LAist Studios
why: Based on an acclaimed nonfiction book; praised for cinematic sound production.
description: True-crime narrative documentary based on Peter Houlahan's book, reconstructing one of the most violent bank robberies in American history and its lasting effect on U.S. policing.

title: Bundyville
author: Oregon Public Broadcasting
why: Named one of the best podcasts of 2018 by Apple and The Atlantic; National Magazine Award finalist for podcasting.

title: The Coming Storm

why: Widely acclaimed for tracing the roots of American conspiracy theory; British Podcast Award nominee

title: Death in Ice Valley
author: BBC World Service
why: Landmark BBC/NRK co-production; helped identify new leads in a 50-year-old cold case, British Podcast Award winner
description: Journalists Marit Higraff (NRK) and Neil McCarthy (BBC) reopen the 1970 case of the unidentified 'Isdal Woman,' found burned to death in a Norwegian valley, digging into Cold War espionage theories.

title: Intrigue
author: BBC Radio 4
why: One of BBC Sounds' flagship narrative-doc strands; multiple seasons have won British Podcast Awards
description: An anthology strand of stranger-than-fiction investigative sagas — con artists, spies, and hidden agendas — with each season a self-contained deep-dive led by a different BBC journalist.

title: The Missing Cryptoqueen
author: BBC Sounds
network: BBC Radio 4 / BBC Sounds / BBC World Service
why: One of BBC's biggest podcast hits; credited with reigniting the FBI hunt for Ignatova; multiple award wins
description: Jamie Bartlett investigates Dr Ruja Ignatova, the 'Cryptoqueen' who vanished after allegedly swindling billions from investors worldwide with the fake cryptocurrency OneCoin, and the global manhunt to find her.

title: Fairy Meadow
author: BBC Radio 4
why: BBC Radio 4 narrative-doc praised for sensitive handling of a decades-old case
description: A narrative investigation into a horrific 1980s cold case in an Australian seaside town, and the decades-long fight for justice by the victim's family.

title: Untold: The Daniel Morgan Murder
author: Duende Productions and Flameflower Studios
why: Tied to the landmark Daniel Morgan Independent Panel report into Met Police corruption
description: An anthology strand digging into Britain's most disturbing unsolved cases and institutional scandals, beginning with the axe-murder of private investigator Daniel Morgan and the alleged police corruption that buried it.

title: 13 Minutes to the Moon
author: BBC World Service
why: Multi-award-winning; British Podcast Award and Webby honors for its Apollo 11 season
description: Presented by Kevin Fong (and later Maggie Aderin-Pocock/Tim Peake), a season-by-season narrative history of the space race, from Apollo 11's final descent to Apollo 13's near-disaster and Artemis II.

title: File on 4 Investigates
author: BBC Radio 4
why: One of BBC's longest-running investigative journalism programmes, Sony/ARIA award nominee history
description: BBC's long-running investigative journalism strand, each episode a self-contained reported documentary exposing corporate wrongdoing, institutional failure, or hidden scandal.

title: Fake Heiress
author: BBC Radio 4
why: Early narrative doc on the Anna Delvey story, predating the Netflix dramatization
description: The story of Anna Sorokin (aka Anna Delvey), the Russian-German con artist who posed as a wealthy heiress to infiltrate elite New York social and art circles.

title: The Battersea Poltergeist
author: BBC Radio 4
why: Critically praised as an innovative hybrid of drama and documentary; British Podcast Award winner
description: A genre-blurring dramatized-documentary reconstruction of a real reported 1950s haunting in a South London council house, told through archive and testimony.

title: The Lovecraft Investigations
author: BBC Radio 4
why: Acclaimed audio-drama/doc hybrid; multiple award nominations for innovative sound design
description: A scripted narrative-fiction-as-journalism series recasting H.P. Lovecraft's mythos as modern investigative podcast reports, blending faux-documentary techniques with horror fiction.

title: Bed of Lies
author: The Telegraph
why: BBC 5 Live narrative-doc exploring romance fraud ahead of the broader catfishing podcast wave
description: An investigation into 'romance fraud' and coercive-control cases where victims were manipulated and defrauded by partners posing as someone else entirely.

title: Sirens
author: Nayely Estrada-Gonzalez
why: Reexamines a landmark British policing failure with a survivor- and victim-centered lens
description: A narrative history investigating the 1980s Yorkshire Ripper police manhunt failures and the culture that let Peter Sutcliffe evade capture for years while killing women.

title: The Assassination
author: BBC World Service
why: BBC World Service international investigative-narrative strand
description: A reported narrative investigation into a real political assassination, tracing conspiracy, cover-up, and the hunt for truth across international borders.

title: Fake Diana: Case of the Missing Blue Diamond
author: Crawlspace Media
why: BBC World Service heist-investigation narrative with international reporting scope
description: A globe-spanning investigation into an elaborate diamond heist and the international network of thieves and fences behind it.

title: Uncover
author: unCoVer
why: One of CBC's flagship podcast franchises with 30+ seasons; Bomb on Board won the RTDNA Canada award for Best Original/Enterprise Journalism (2019); regularly charts internationally.
description: Anthology of true-crime and investigative-journalism seasons, each hosted by a different journalist embedded in the story — cult escapes, foiled terror plots, wrongful convictions, and cold cases.

title: Someone Knows Something
author: CBC
why: Long-running, critically acclaimed cold-case series credited in some seasons with contributing new leads to real investigations; multiple award nominations.
description: Documentarian David Ridgen partners with families of the missing and murdered to re-investigate unsolved Canadian cold cases in real time, working alongside witnesses, suspects and sometimes police.

title: Missing & Murdered
author: Gabriella
why: Peabody Award winner; widely credited with reshaping national conversation on missing and murdered Indigenous women; host Connie Walker later won a Pulitzer Prize for related reporting elsewhere.
description: Journalist Connie Walker investigates the unsolved disappearance and murder of Indigenous women, exposing police and institutional failures across two seasons.

title: Alone: A Love Story
author: CBC
why: Widely praised scripted memoir podcast; nominated for/won multiple Canadian podcast awards.
description: Radio producer Michelle Parise narrates a scripted memoir about the collapse of her marriage and the messy, unglamorous years of rebuilding a life alone.

title: The Con
author: Zac Stuart-Pontier and On Air Fest
why: Praised for tightly-plotted true-crime storytelling; part of CBC's growing scam-and-fraud narrative slate.

title: Personally
author: Personally
why: Part of CBC's prestige narrative-memoir slate alongside Alone: A Love Story.

title: Recall: How to Start a Revolution
author: CBC
why: Released for the 50th anniversary of the October Crisis; praised for immersive sound design and rare archival access.
description: Scripted history documentary revisiting Quebec's 1970 October Crisis — FLQ kidnappings, the War Measures Act, soldiers in the streets — told through bombers, survivors and officials who lived it.

title: Crime Story
author: CBC
why: One of CBC's flagship true-crime aggregation feeds, regularly charting on Canadian podcast charts.
description: A curated true-crime documentary feed presenting longform investigative and narrative crime stories, blending CBC originals with select international true-crime documentaries.

title: The Detectives
author: Zerp
why: Long-running CBC Radio staple noted for its unusually raw use of primary-source audio rather than reenactment.
description: Real Canadian criminal investigations reconstructed almost entirely from actual police interrogation tapes, wiretaps and courtroom evidence, letting detectives and suspects tell the story in their own words.

title: On Drugs
author: CBC
why: Praised for blending narrative history with science journalism; nominated for Canadian podcast awards.
description: Scripted documentary anthology tracing the surprising, often bizarre histories of individual drugs — from caffeine to cocaine to opioids — and how they reshaped society.

title: The Fifth Estate
author: Torch Of Liberty Media Foundation
why: One of the most decorated investigative journalism programs in Canadian broadcast history.
description: Canada's longest-running investigative documentary program, adapted into podcast form; each episode is a deeply reported investigation into corporate wrongdoing, institutional failure or crime.

title: Ideas
author: CBC
why: One of the longest-running and most influential documentary radio programs in the world; international radio award recognition.
description: Long-running documentary program producing scripted, often single-narrator deep dives into history, science, philosophy and culture, many structured as narrative history documentaries.

title: The Teacher's Pet
author: The Australian
why: One of the most downloaded true-crime podcasts globally; directly credited with prompting the reinvestigation and eventual 2022 murder conviction of Chris Dawson; Walkley and Gold Quill Award winner.

title: Bronwyn

why: From the team behind The Teacher's Pet; renewed police interest in the case.

title: Shandee's Story
author: The Australian
why: Prompted a coronial inquest into the original police investigation.
description: Hedley Thomas investigates the unsolved 2013 stabbing murder of Shandee Blackburn in Mackay, Queensland, and the trial that ended in acquittal.

title: The Teacher's Trial
author: The Australian
description: Follow-up series to The Teacher's Pet, chronicling the murder trial and conviction of Chris Dawson for the killing of his wife Lynette.

title: Who The Hell Is Hamish?
author: The Australian
why: Broke a major fraud story that led to McLaren's arrest and prosecution.
description: Reporter David Murray unravels the double life of Hamish McLaren, a charismatic con man who posed as a globe-trotting hedge-fund trader to swindle women he met on dating apps out of millions.

title: Expanse
author: ABC Australia
description: ABC RN's narrative documentary anthology strand exploring true crimes, mysteries, and unsolved cases from regional and outback Australia.

title: Wrong Skin
author: The Age and Sydney Morning Herald
why: Credited with renewing national attention on the Bowraville murders and police failures in investigating Indigenous deaths.
description: Journalist Allan Clarke investigates the 1987 disappearance and murder of two Aboriginal teenagers, Colleen Walker and Clinton Speedy-Duroux, in Bowraville, NSW, and the decades-long fight by their families for justice.

title: 7am
author: Solstice Media
why: One of Australia's most popular daily news podcasts, from the publisher of The Monthly and The Saturday Paper.
description: A daily narrative news podcast in which a single reported story — on Australian politics, power, and current affairs — is told in long-form by a journalist each morning.

title: Background Briefing
author: ABC Australia
why: Decades-long institution of Australian investigative audio journalism; regular Walkley Award winner.
description: ABC RN's flagship investigative documentary program; each episode is a deeply reported feature exposing corporate wrongdoing, institutional failure, or hidden social issues.

title: Earshot
author: WXXI News
description: A long-running narrative documentary strand telling immersive, character-driven true stories through rich sound design, spanning crime, social issues, and personal history.

title: Days Like These
author: ABC Australia
description: A This American Life-style narrative nonfiction show built from true personal stories submitted by everyday Australians, produced with cinematic sound design.

title: Casefile True Crime
author: Casefile Presents
why: One of the most popular true-crime podcasts worldwide, regularly charting in the global top 10.
description: Anonymous-narrator series delivering meticulously researched, single-episode deep dives into true crime cases worldwide, from serial killers to bizarre unsolved mysteries.

title: Fallout
author: Robots Radio
why: One of Australia's few major narrative sports-scandal podcasts.
description: A narrative documentary investigating the Essendon Football Club supplements scandal, in which players were injected with an experimental, potentially banned substance under a systemic doping program.

title: Bondi Badlands
author: The Age and Sydney Morning Herald
why: Contributed to renewed police and public attention on the Sydney gay-hate murders inquiry.
description: Host Patrick Abboud investigates a decades-long spate of unsolved anti-gay hate crimes and suspicious deaths at Sydney's Bondi cliffs from the 1970s to 2000s, and survivors' fight for a full police reinvestigation.

title: The Night Driver
author: The Australian
why: Reclassified from unknown: LiSTNR/News Corp narrative podcasts are distributed free, ad-supported, on all major apps like other iHeart/indie shows.
description: A narrative true-crime series reconstructing the years-long manhunt for a serial rapist terrorising Melbourne's western suburbs in the 1990s and the detectives who pursued him.

title: Fool Me Once
author: Marist and Andrew Lamson
why: Standalone-notable season of the Unravel anthology.
description: Investigates an elaborate, long-running psychic con that manipulated and defrauded a family over years, exposing the mechanics of coercive control by a self-styled clairvoyant.

title: Scam Inc
author: The Economist

title: The Prince
author: The Economist

title: Boss Class
author: The Economist
why: Feed note: free with ads on public RSS; Economist Podcasts+ unlocks ad-free/early access.
description: Narrative-driven exploration of management and workplace power dynamics through real case studies and reporting.

title: Hot Money: The New Narcos
author: Pushkin Industries & Financial Times
why: FT's Hot Money franchise won acclaim for blending financial journalism with narrative crime storytelling.
